title: Increment/Decrement Operators
description: Increase or decrease values by one.

slides.title: Adding or Subtracting 1
slides.content:
# Increment and Decrement

PHP provides handy operators to increase or decrease a variable by 1.

*   `++$x` тАУ Pre-increment: increment $x by 1, then return $x
*   `$x++` тАУ Post-increment: return $x, then increment $x
*   `--$x` тАУ Pre-decrement
*   `$x--` тАУ Post-decrement

They work on numbers and also on strings (increment only).

slides.title: Pre vs Post
Incrementing Strings
slides.content:
## Fun Fact: String Increment

PHP supports incrementing strings in a Perl-like way:

```php
<?php
$str = 'a';
echo ++$str; // 'b'

$str2 = 'z';
echo ++$str2; // 'aa'

$str3 = 'ABC';
echo ++$str3; // 'ABD'
?>
```

But decrement doesn't work on strings.

quizData.explanation: Post-increment returns the original value (3) to $y, but then increments $x to 4. So final $x = 4.
